mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate reduces discount rates and supports equity valuations, so a sustained decline in policy rates predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is confirmed when rate cuts are broad-based rather than offset by rising inflation.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive on average after FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-month window, or if the signal's hit rate falls below 55% in easing episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates above the recent trend while FEDFUNDS stays elevated, the market prices a higher path of real rates and tighter liquidity, which should pressure S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change rises above its prior 12-month median and FEDFUNDS is at or above 3.5%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not show negative 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the average triggered return is not at least 1 percentage point below unconditional 20-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates weakening household income growth and deteriorating earnings expectations, so a month-over-month increase in unemployment should predict lower S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.2 percentage points over 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are not negative in episodes where UNRATE rises by more than 0.2 percentage points over 2 months, or if the effect vanishes after controlling for market trend.

mechanism_narrative: A broad increase in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates stronger payroll income and retail spending, which supports revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is strongest when both series improve together rather than separately.
failure_condition: Reject if combined PAYEMS and RSAFS improvement does not correspond to above-average 20-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term is not positive in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO signals stronger industrial output and better cyclical earnings momentum, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next week. The effect is a short-horizon confirmation signal rather than a long lead indicator.
failure_condition: Reject if one-week forward SPX returns fail to improve after positive INDPRO changes, or if the sign of the relationship turns negative in rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp slowdown in HOUST reflects weakening housing demand and tighter credit transmission, which should forecast weaker equity returns over the next quarter. The signal is valid only when housing starts are falling from an already moderate level, not during rebound phases.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 8% over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns remain positive in most episodes of a >8% 3-month decline in HOUST, or if the signal is dominated by recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth slows while UNRATE rises, labor income momentum deteriorates and consumption expectations weaken, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The mechanism requires both payroll deceleration and unemployment worsening to be present together.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average growth turns negative and UNRATE rises at least 0.1 percentage points in the same 2-month window
failure_condition: Reject if the combined labor-stress trigger does not predict negative 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if PAYEMS and UNRATE effects are independently sufficient without interaction.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronous improvement in GDPC1 and RSAFS indicates that aggregate demand and nominal spending are both strengthening, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal fails when GDP growth is driven by inventory or temporary statistical noise without matching retail sales growth.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 rises on the latest quarterly release and RSAFS is above its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if concurrent GDPC1 and RSAFS strength does not produce positive 60-day SPX returns on average, or if the return edge disappears when GDP revisions are incorporated.
